document.title: Software Validation Report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: Content of Premarket Submissions for Device Software Functions (June 14, 2023)
Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices (May 11, 2005)

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software validation report for GT1717C and GT1717CP Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector software and firmware.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by describing software requirements, architecture, verification, validation, hazard analysis, traceability, versioning, and unresolved anomalies for the detector software.
keyPoints: The GT1717C and GT1717CP differ in internal CsI deposition thickness, but the software and firmware are the same.
The software is structured from G-series software architecture and includes SDK and onboard firmware/FPGA modules.
The document concludes a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software.
Module verification and system validation results are recorded as pass for listed software, firmware, FPGA, and error-control tests.
The unresolved anomalies section states: None.

concepts.name: Software Level of Concern
concepts.evidence: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software. Level of Concern: Moderate

concepts.name: Software Development Kit
concepts.pageTarget: Software Development Kit for Detector Image Acquisition
concepts.evidence: SDK (Software Development Kit): A collection of functions that acquire detector images and perform various manipulations.

concepts.name: Onboard Module
concepts.pageTarget: Detector Onboard Firmware and FPGA Module
concepts.evidence: Onboard Module (Firmware/FPGA): This program, embedded in the detector, communicates with the PC console and acquires images from the detector's internal FPGA connected to the panel

concepts.name: Calibration
concepts.evidence: Calibration performs following compensations to acquired images.

concepts.name: Offset Correction
concepts.evidence: This correction process makes dark signal offset uniform, and enables to get uniform image.

concepts.name: Gain Correction
concepts.evidence: This process reduces brightness variations across the entire image and achieves a uniform background brightness.

concepts.name: Defect Correction
concepts.evidence: extract corresponding area and compensate through dust remover and defect correction algorithm.

concepts.evidence: The following chart shows a list of potential hazards that may occur during use of AXD.

concepts.evidence: Unresolved Anomalies No Problem Description Criticality Reason for Problem Potential Impact None

evidence.claim: The document is a Software Validation Report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models GT1717C and GT1717CP.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Validation Report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: GT1717C, GT1717CP
evidence.pageTarget: GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The technical documentation document number is A-SVR-CYN.
The header identifies the document as Rev. No. 2 with Rev. Date April 14, 2026.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-SVR-CYN Rev. No. 2 Software Validation Report Rev. Date April 14, 2026

evidence.claim: The GT1717C and GT1717CP have different internal CsI deposition thickness, but the same software and firmware.
evidence.evidenceText: The difference between the GT1717C and GT1717CP products is the internal CsI deposition thickness, but the software and firmware are the same.

evidence.claim: The document concludes the AXD software has a Moderate level of concern.
evidence.evidenceText: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software. Level of Concern: Moderate

evidence.claim: The level-of-concern section cites FDA software guidance documents from June 14, 2023 and May 11, 2005.
evidence.evidenceText: FDA guidance document entitled “Content of Premarket Submissions for Device Software Functions” (June 14, 2023) (“Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices”, May 11, 2005.)
evidence.pageTarget: FDA Device Software Premarket Submission Guidance

evidence.claim: The detector software includes an SDK and an Onboard Module with firmware and FPGA functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector software can be broadly divided into two categories. 1. SDK (Software Development Kit) ... 2. Onboard Module (Firmware/FPGA)

evidence.claim: The SDK includes common functions, image acquisition, calibration, and firmware update modules.
evidence.evidenceText: Common functions module: Control detector setup; Image acquisition module: Control image read; Calibration module: Calibration setup and correction or pre-processing control of image; Firmware update: Write detector firmware update file and read information

evidence.claim: The onboard module includes CPU module and FPGA app module roles.
evidence.evidenceText: CPU Module: System initialization and task creation. And power device and shock sensor communication control and initialization; FPGA App Module: Image reading and communication message processing in FPGA
evidence.pageTarget: Detector Onboard Firmware and FPGA Module

evidence.claim: The listed PC system requirements are Windows 10 or higher, i5 8th generation or higher, and 8GB DDR4 RAM or higher.
evidence.evidenceText: System requirement OS: Windows 10 or higher CPU: i5 8th generation or higher RAM: 8GB DDR4 or higher

evidence.claim: The detector hardware requirements include Intel Cyclone V, 2GB volatile memory, 8GB flash memory, network support, and Embedded Linux 32-bit.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector H/W Device requirement CPU: Intel Cyclone V Volatile Memory: 2GB Flash Memory: 8GB NIC: 10/100/1000 network system, 802.11 a/b/g/n/ac. OS: Embedded Linux 32-bit

evidence.claim: The development language is Microsoft Visual Professional 2022, C#.
evidence.evidenceText: Program Language Microsoft Visual Professional 2022, C#
evidence.pageTarget: GT Series Detector Software Development Environment

evidence.claim: The development process is described as fulfilling 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The development process fulfils the requirements 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.

evidence.claim: The software development process follows a classic waterfall methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process at software follows a classic waterfall methodology

evidence.claim: Module verification positively evaluated successful installation on a standard PC.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of module verification, we positively evaluate the success in installed Software on standard PC.

evidence.claim: System testing found all functions configured in each module adequate and no unresolved safety or efficiency bugs.
evidence.evidenceText: System testing verifies that all functions configured in each module are adequate. No unresolved bugs have been found in this software that affect safety and efficiency.

evidence.claim: The hazard analysis includes communication error, x-ray exposure energy, firmware, shock, parameter, sensor, login, and data encryption hazards.
evidence.evidenceText: Hazard Analysis No. Hazard ... Communication ... x-ray exposure energy ... Firmware ... Shock ... Parameter ... Sensor ... Login ... Data encryption

evidence.claim: Unresolved anomalies are listed as none.
evidence.evidenceText: Unresolved Anomalies No Problem Description Criticality Reason for Problem Potential Impact None

reviewFlags.reason: Revision metadata is conflicting: headers show Rev. No. 2 and Rev. Date April 14, 2026, while the title page states Report No. A-SVR-CYN (Rev.1) and later pages show File No. A-SVR-CYN, Rev. No. 1, Rev. Date March 23, 2026.
Authority is ambiguous: source metadata indicates MFDS/domestic, but the source text cites FDA guidance and 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls and does not explicitly mention MFDS.
The document references CYAN through source metadata and firmware version name cyan.dll, but CYAN is not clearly defined as a product model in the source text.

Several extracted tables contain OCR or formatting inconsistencies, including shifted page headers, repeated Rev. No. 1 pages, and traceability tags such as SV2-17 and SV2-7000 that do not align cleanly with earlier test numbering.
